records.ety: [OE. bouel, bouele, OF. boel, boele, F. boyau, fr. L. botellus a small sausage, in LL. also intestine, dim. of L. botulus sausage.]

defs: One of the intestines of an animal; an entrail, especially of man; a gut; -- generally used in the plural.
quotes: He burst asunder in the midst, and all his bowels gushed out.

defs: Hence, figuratively: The interior part of anything; as, the bowels of the earth.
quotes: His soldiers . . . cried out amain, And rushed into the bowels of the battle.

defs: The seat of pity or kindness. Hence: Tenderness; compassion.
quotes: Bloody Bonner, that corpulent tyrant, full (as one said) of guts, and empty of bowels.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Boweled or Bowelled (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Boweling or Bowelling.]

defs: To take out the bowels of; to eviscerate; to disembowel.
